message:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry: register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) functions, a module-level filter registry dict, and updates to Prompt._template_from_str and Prompt._template_from_file (the env.filters assignment block) so global filters are merged in (local filters take precedence over global ones).

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom Jinja2 filters support: modify prompt() decorator to accept optional filters param (support both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage), Prompt.from_str() and Prompt.from_file() to accept filters param, new create_jinja_env() helper to centralize env creation+filter registration, and refactor _template_from_str()/_template_from_file() to use it.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: create_jinja_env() helper
code: def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create a Jinja2 environment with default, global and local filters registered."""
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
note: _FILTERS is my global registry dict (module-level, above this function). Local filters param still wins since it is applied last. Placed above Prompt class alongside get_fn_name etc., or wherever you prefer - let me know if you want it elsewhere.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: create_jinja_env() helper + _template_from_str/_template_from_file + filter registry
code: _global_filters: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, filter_func: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a filter globally so it is available in all prompt templates."""
    _global_filters[name] = filter_func


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a previously registered global filter."""
    _global_filters.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create a Jinja2 environment with the default outlines settings and filters.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
    filters
        Custom filters to register on top of the default outlines filters and
        any globally registered filters (via `register_filter`). Locally
        provided filters take precedence over globally registered filters,
        which take precedence over the default outlines filters.

    Returns
    -------
    A `jinja2.Environment` instance.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_global_filters)
    if filters is not None:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
note: Proposing exact names: _global_filters dict, register_filter/unregister_filter functions, create_jinja_env(loader, filters). _template_from_str/_template_from_file will call create_jinja_env(None, filters) and create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(dir), filters) respectively. Let me know if you already committed to different names.

    """Create a Jinja2 environment with the default outlines settings and filters.

        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,

    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_global_filters)

    return env

code: _global_filters: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, filter_func: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a filter globally so it is available in all prompt templates."""
    _global_filters[name] = filter_func


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a previously registered global filter."""
    _global_filters.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create a Jinja2 environment with the default outlines settings and filters.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
    filters
        Custom filters to register on top of the default outlines filters and
        any globally registered filters (via `register_filter`). Locally
        provided filters take precedence over globally registered filters,
        which take precedence over the default outlines filters.

    Returns
    -------
    A `jinja2.Environment` instance.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_global_filters)
    if filters is not None:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
note: Heads up: your last AGREE (ts 1783537195) echoed an older/incomplete version (_FILTERS name, no register_filter/unregister_filter, no param defaults) - that must have crossed in transit with your own follow-up DRAFT. Please use THIS block verbatim instead (identical to what I sent in my prior AGREE) - it's your combined draft with _global_filters/register_filter/unregister_filter included. Confirmed on your plan: _template_from_str calls create_jinja_env(None, filters), _template_from_file calls create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters). The filters param on Prompt.from_str/from_file/prompt decorator are your exclusive constructs - no overlap concern from me there, go ahead.

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: _template_from_str and _template_from_file classmethods
code:     @classmethod
    def _template_from_str(
        _, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        # Dedent, and remove extra linebreak
        cleaned_template = inspect.cleandoc(content)

        # Add linebreak if there were any extra linebreaks that
        # `cleandoc` would have removed
        ends_with_linebreak = content.replace(" ", "").endswith("\n\n")
        if ends_with_linebreak:
            cleaned_template += "\n"

        # Remove extra whitespaces, except those that immediately follow a newline symbol.
        # This is necessary to avoid introducing whitespaces after backslash `\` characters
        # used to continue to the next line without linebreak.
        cleaned_template = re.sub(r"(?![\r\n])(\b\s+)", " ", cleaned_template)

        env = create_jinja_env(None, filters)

        return env.from_string(cleaned_template)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_file(
        _, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        file_directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
        env = create_jinja_env(jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters)
        return env.get_template(os.path.basename(path))
note: filters param here is my addition (custom filters passed by the caller of prompt()/from_str()/from_file()); it flows into create_jinja_env as the local filters arg. Your global registry is picked up automatically inside create_jinja_env regardless of this param. Let me know if this matches what you need on your end.

Since we're both inserting this identical block, it needs to land at the exact same location in both patches (same surrounding context) or git will duplicate it. Proposal: place it directly after the imports, right before 'class Prompt:' (i.e. right after line 12 'import pydantic', before the blank lines and @dataclass). Confirm or propose alternative placement.
